channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.title: Strategy’s Bitcoin Buying Spree Resumes With Fresh 535 BTC Accumulation

item.title: Your Purple Team Isn't Purple — It's Just Red and Blue in the Same Room

item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 'TrustFall' Convention Exposes Claude Code Execution Risk
item.description: Malicious repositories can trigger code execution in Claude Code, Cursor CLI, Gemini CLI, and CoPilot CLI with minimal or no user interaction, thanks to skimpy warning dialogs....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: AI-Driven Cyberattack on Mexico Couldn't Breach OT Systems
item.description: The most sophisticated AI-integrated campaign to date hit a brick wall in the form of a SCADA login screen....

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Edge Plaintext Passwords, ICS 0-Days, Patch-or-Die Alerts and 25+ New Stories
item.description: Bad week. Turns out the easiest way to get hacked in 2026 is still the same old garbage: shady packages, fake apps, forgotten DNS junk, scam ads, and stolen logins getting dumped into Discord channels...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Cybersecurity Stolen ChipSoft claims patient data confirmed destroyed following cyberattack
item.description: There’s an update to the ChipSoft ransomware attack. DigitalShield reports that although ChipSoft hasn’t revealed whether it paid Embargo ransom, it did disclose that some negotiations had occurred. ...
item.dc:creator: DataBreaches.net

item.title: Day Zero Readiness: The Operational Gaps That Break Incident Response
item.description: Having an incident response retainer, or even a pre-approved external incident response firm, is not the same as being ready for an incident. A retainer means someone will answer the phone. Operationa...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: TrustedVolumes hit by $6.7M exploit as 1inch denies breach
item.description: 1inch said its protocols, infrastructure and user funds were not affected by the exploit targeting independent resolver TrustedVolumes....
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph - Security

item.title: vm2 Node.js Library Vulnerabilities Enable Sandbox Escape and Arbitrary Code Execution
item.description: A dozen critical security vulnerabilities have been disclosed in the vm2 Node.js library that could be exploited by bad actors to break out of the sandbox and execute arbitrary code on susceptible sys...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ‘GothFerrari’ Sentenced to 78 Months in Prison for Role in Massive Cryptocurrency Heist

item.title: VoidStealer Malware Darts Past Google Chrome's Encryption
item.description: Authors of the VoidStealer Trojan uncovered yet another way to get around Google's App-Bound Encryption (ABE), opening the door to infostealers....

item.title: Instructure Breach Exposes Schools' Vendor Dependence
item.description: ShinyHunters' attack on Instructure, which owns the widely used Canvas learning management system (LMS), carries big questions about the trust educational institutions put into their vendors....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Mirai-Based xlabs_v1 Botnet Exploits ADB to Hijack IoT Devices for DDoS Attacks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have exposed a new Mirai-derived botnet that self-identifies as xlabs_v1 and targets internet-exposed devices running Android Debug Bridge (ADB) to enlist them in a network c...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Missouri regulators escalate pressure on Conduent over data breach potentially affecting millions
item.description: Sarah Motter reports: Missouri regulators say a major national vendor is stonewalling their investigation into a cybersecurity breach that could affect millions of consumers. The Missouri Department o...
